title: E001: Add Keystone API types, webhooks & CRD generation
summary: Review of post-external-feedback changes: CEL XValidation rule fix (self.rules != null → size(self.rules) > 0), KeystoneWebhook opt-out from deepcopy generation (+kubebuilder:object:generate=false), and two new review pattern files. Both external review comments addressed correctly. One blocking issue: CRD YAML not regenerated after the CEL rule change.

tests_checklist.item: Tests exist and pass (make test OPERATOR=keystone)
Edge cases covered in webhook tests
No test changes needed for this diff (CEL rule + codegen marker)

code_quality_checklist.item: Minimal, targeted changes with no scope creep
Review pattern files well-structured and actionable

security_checklist.item: CEL rule now correctly rejects empty maps, closing admission gap
No hardcoded secrets or sensitive data

architecture_checklist.item: Non-API struct correctly opted out of deepcopy generation
CEL semantics now match webhook semantics exactly

fail_fast_checklist.item: Empty map rules now rejected at CRD schema level before reaching webhook

defensive_checklist.item: Defense-in-depth: CEL + webhook both reject empty rules map

issues_found.description: CRD YAML not regenerated after CEL rule change in keystone_types.go. Running `make manifests` produces a 1-line diff: the committed CRD still has `rule: self.rules != null || self.configMapRef != null` but should have `rule: size(self.rules) > 0 || self.configMapRef != null`. Generated files must be in sync with Go type definitions.
issues_found.fix: Run `make manifests` and commit the updated CRD YAML file `operators/keystone/config/crd/bases/keystone.openstack.c5c3.io_keystones.yaml`.

next_steps: Run `make manifests` and commit the regenerated CRD YAML to bring generated files in sync with the CEL rule change
